result:
I’d make it so people could directly feel the real consequences of their actions on others—just enough to understand, not enough to overwhelm.

Not mind-reading, not constant empathy overload. More like a brief, undeniable glimpse: if you lie, you feel the confusion and erosion of trust it creates; if you’re kind, you feel the steadiness and relief it brings. A built-in feedback loop that can’t be ignored or rationalized away.

A lot of what goes wrong in the world isn’t because people *want* harm in some grand, cartoonish way—it’s because distance makes it easy to abstract, justify, or just not think too hard. Close that distance, even a little, and a surprising number of decisions start to change on their own.

It wouldn’t fix everything. Some people would still choose harm. But far fewer could do it casually, or pretend it doesn’t matter.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
